X-Axis : X axis parameters

# setMaxX
Set the maximum value for data in the X axis
Type: float
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

setMaxX : null

setMaxX : 75


# setMaxX2
Set the maximum value for data in the second X axis
Type: float
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

setMaxX2 : null

setMaxX2 : 75


# setMinX
Set the minimum value for data in the X axis
Type: float
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

setMinX : null

setMinX : 25


# setMinX2
Set the minimum value for data in the second X axis
Type: float
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

setMinX2 : 75


# setPanningMaxX
Set the maximum value allowed to pann the data in the X axis
Type: float
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

setPanningMaxX : null

setPanningMaxX : 125


# setPanningMinX
Set the minimum value allowed to pann the data in the X axis
Type: float
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

# xAxis
Name of the samples, groups or variables to be displayed in the X axis
Type: array
Default: []
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

xAxis : ["Col1", "Col2"]

xAxis : ["Col3", "Col4"]


# xAxis2
Name of the samples, groups or variables to be displayed in the second X axis
Type: array
Default: []
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

xAxis2 : ["Col2", "Col3"]

xAxis2 : ["Col3", "Col4"]


# xAxis2Rotate
Flag to rotate 180 degrees the direction of the X axis on the top
Type: boolean
Default: false
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

# xAxis2Show
Flag to show/hide the X axis on the top
Type: boolean
Default: false
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

xAxis2Show : true

xAxis2Show : false


# xAxis2TickFormat
X axis format for the tick values in the second X axis. It could be a string with the sprintf format or an array where the first parameter is the sprintf format and the second parameter is a modification applied to the value before is passed to the sprintf function, i.e. ("/1000", "+ 100", etc.)
Type: string
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

xAxis2TickFormat : "%.0f Mil."

xAxis2TickFormat : "%.2f"


# xAxis2Ticks
Approx number of tick lines in the second X axis
Type: integer
Default: 5
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

X axis2 title on the top
Type: string
Default:

# xAxisCeilValue
Value to ceil the data in the X axis
Type: float
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

xAxisCeilValue : 100


# xAxisCurrent
Sample id that is currently displayed in the X axis. Important when there are more that one series displayed in the graph
Type: integer
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

# xAxisExact
Flag to force exact values in X axis
Type: boolean
Default: false
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

Value to factor the data in the X axis

Value to floor the data in the X axis
Type: float

# xAxisGridMajorColor
Color for the major grid lines in the X axis on the bottom
Type: color
Default: rgb(204, 204, 204)
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

# xAxisGridMajorLineType
Type of line for the major grid lines in the X axis on the bottom
Type: string
Default: solid
Options: rect, solid, spline, dotted, dashed, dotdash, longdash, twodash
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

# xAxisTickFormat
X axis format for the tick values in the X axis. It could be a string with the sprintf format or an array where the first parameter is the sprintf format and the second parameter is a modification applied to the value before is passed to the sprintf function, i.e. ("/1000", "+ 100", etc.)
Type: string
Default:
Used in: Area, AreaLine, Bar, BarLine, Boxplot, Bubble, Bullet, Chord, Circular, Contour, Correlation, Density, DotLine, Dotplot, Dumbbell, Fish, Gantt, Heatmap, Histogram, Line, Lollipop, Meter, ParallelCoordinates, Pie, Radar, Sankey, Scatter2D, Scatter3D, ScatterBubble2D, Stacked, StackedLine, StackedPercent, StackedPercentLine, Streamgraph, Sunburst, TagCloud, Tree, Treemap, Violin, Waterfall

xAxisTickFormat : "%.0f"

xAxisTickFormat : "%.2f"

CanvasXpress a stand-alone JavaScript Library for Data Analytics. Built for the purpose of reproducible research with a sophisticated and unobtrusive user interface. Full and effortless audit trail of data, configuration and all user interactions in every visualization.
